rows.Symptom: Chest pain/pressure, pain radiating to arm/jaw
rows.Action: Stop immediately; advise seeking emergency medical care now

rows.Symptom: Shortness of breath disproportionate to effort, or at rest
Sudden numbness/weakness one side of body, slurred speech
rows.Action: Stop immediately; advise emergency care (stroke signs)

rows.Symptom: Severe, sudden joint deformity or inability to bear weight after injury
Signs of anaphylaxis (swelling, hives, breathing difficulty)

rows.Programming Response: Remove exercise/pattern entirely; do not substitute with "lighter version" of same pattern; recommend professional evaluation before reintroducing

rows.Pain Pattern: Pain only at end-range of motion
rows.Programming Response: Temporarily cap ROM, add mobility work, re-test in 1–2 weeks

rows.Pain Pattern: Pain that increases session over session despite substitution
rows.Programming Response: Full pattern removal + professional referral flag, do not keep attempting substitutions

rows.Hard Constraints: Avoid max-effort Valsalva lifts; cap RPE at 8; longer rest; avoid extended breath-holds
Monitor for hypoglycemia signs; recommend blood glucose check before/after if on insulin/sulfonylureas; avoid unsupervised fasted high-intensity work
Avoid overheating, avoid new high-impact activity, continue prior training within RPE ≤7
No supine exercises after ~20 weeks (per client/physician guidance), avoid high fall-risk movements, avoid heavy Valsalva, reduce max intensity
No loaded core/impact work until cleared; pelvic floor considerations
Avoid spinal flexion under load (e.g., loaded sit-ups, high-rep loaded crunches), avoid high-impact jumping without clearance
Follow physician/PT ROM and load restrictions explicitly; never exceed disclosed ROM limits
No caloric deficit prescription, no body-composition tracking metrics pushed, route to supportive/professional referral (Section 6)
Ensure warm-up ramp, flag conditioning intensity caps, recommend inhaler availability during sessions
`medical_clearance_required` mandatory, cap intensity at clearance-specified level only

rows.Programming Note: Do not use HR-based intensity zones; use RPE instead
Emphasize hydration reminders, caution in heat
Conservative loading progression, avoid rapid load jumps

rows.Rule: No specific calorie/macro targets
rows.Detail: If active ED disclosed or behavioral flags present, system must not output numeric diet targets anywhere downstream (file 7)

rows.Rule: No body-composition emphasis
rows.Detail: Suppress progress photos/measurement prompts; shift check-ins to performance/energy/sleep metrics only

rows.Rule: No causal psychological narratives
rows.Detail: System never states *why* a client restricts/binges; reflects only what is disclosed

rows.Rule: Always suggest professional support
rows.Detail: Every ED-flagged interaction includes a suggestion to involve a doctor, dietitian, or therapist, without diagnosing

rows.Overlay: No max-effort testing, emphasize technique mastery, cap external load progression rate, guardian visibility into program

Mandatory fall-risk screen before any single-leg/unstable-surface work; extra warm-up time; conservative RPE cap (≤7) until baseline established; bone density consideration default to caution (treat as `osteoporosis_risk_unknown` unless screened negative)

rows.Signal: Client reports exercising in extreme heat/humidity without acclimation
rows.Response: Reduce intensity, increase rest, hydration emphasis, recommend indoor alternative

rows.Signal: Client reports exercising alone with heavy free-weight max attempts
rows.Response: Recommend spotter/safety bars/machine alternative for near-max attempts
Force deload week equivalent (file 10 Section 3), no high-intensity work until 48h symptom-free

rows.Edge Case: Client discloses emergency symptom mid-session via check-in text (not live)
rows.Rule: Treat as urgent; response must lead with escalation language before anything else, regardless of what else was asked

rows.Edge Case: Conflicting disclosures (e.g., "cleared by physician" but also lists uncontrolled cardiac condition)
rows.Rule: Trust the higher-risk disclosure; keep `medical_clearance_required` active until explicitly reconciled

rows.Edge Case: Client asks system to "ignore" a flag to get harder programming
rows.Rule: Refuse; flags are not user-overridable except via updated medical clearance input or resolved timeline (Section 7)

rows.Edge Case: Guardian requests removal of teen's safety overlay
rows.Rule: Overlay stays; only age changing or explicit updated professional guidance changes it

rows.Edge Case: Repeated pain reports on unrelated patterns (possible systemic issue)
rows.Rule: After 3 distinct pattern-pain flags in a mesocycle, escalate to full professional re-evaluation recommendation regardless of individual pattern status

rows.Symptom: Safety gate never triggers despite flags present
rows.Cause: Gate not called before generation step
rows.Fix: Enforce `safetyGate()` as mandatory pre-hook in files 1–11, 13

rows.Symptom: ED routing suppressing all nutrition guidance even for unflagged clients
rows.Fix: Require flag confidence threshold (≥2 co-occurring signals) before auto-routing, per Section 6

rows.Programming Response: No axial loading; walking, pain-free mobility only
Bodyweight/light-load hinge and squat drills, no barbell compounds
50% load restart, gradual reintroduction per file 12 Section 7
Standard programming, monitor for recurrence 4 weeks

rows.If Criteria NOT Met (Else Branch): Remain at previous phase, re-check in 48h

rows.Criteria to Advance: 2 sessions of bodyweight/unloaded pattern with pain ≤1/10
rows.If Criteria NOT Met (Else Branch): If pain >1/10 recurs, reset to Step 1 equivalent load, extend timeline

rows.Criteria to Advance: 2 sessions at 50% pre-injury working load, pain ≤1/10
rows.If Criteria NOT Met (Else Branch): If pain recurs, drop back to Step 2, flag for professional eval if this is the 2nd reset

rows.Criteria to Advance: 10-20% load increase per successful pain-free session block
rows.If Criteria NOT Met (Else Branch): If pain recurs at any increase, drop back one step; 2nd recurrence at same step triggers professional referral regardless of severity
Maintain standard monitoring per file 11 Section 6 re-assessment cadence

rows.Edge Case: Client has two simultaneous injuries in different regions
rows.Rule: Apply both protocols independently; if they jointly eliminate all available exercises for a session, fall back to DEFAULT_SAFE_TEMPLATE's unaffected-pattern subset (file 14) rather than skipping the session entirely

rows.Edge Case: Client's reported pain scale contradicts observed/reported function (e.g., "pain scale 8" but reports normal daily activity)
rows.Rule: Flag `self_report_inconsistency`, default to the more conservative (higher-caution) interpretation, request professional eval to reconcile rather than assuming self-report is exaggerated
Stop the affected pattern for remainder of session immediately; do not attempt to "finish the workout" with modified version same-day; reassess protocol at next scheduled session

rows.Edge Case: Client wants to "push through" pain against protocol recommendation
rows.Rule: Do not provide loading progressions that contradict active protocol; may present information neutrally but must not generate a program that violates the current phase's restrictions

rows.Fix: Ensure Section 5 Step 1 re-check condition is actively polled at each check-in (file 11 Section 5), not just at initial injury report

rows.Symptom: Same injury protocol re-triggering every few weeks without recognition of pattern
rows.Fix: Ensure `detectRecurringInjuryPattern()` runs on every new injury report, not just once per client lifetime

rows.Edge Case: Condition well-managed for years, client wants to "stop mentioning it" at every check-in
rows.Rule: Reduce check-in question frequency about it to file 11 Section 6 cadence (not every session) but never fully remove it from `client_state` — long-term training decisions still reference it

rows.Edge Case: Client discloses condition only after several months of training
rows.Rule: Immediately re-run Section 1 routing retroactively; do not treat prior programming as invalidated, just adjust going forward

rows.Edge Case: Two conditions have literally opposite ideal cardio modality recommendations
rows.Rule: Apply Section 4 conservative-resolution rule; surface the tension to the client/coach rather than silently picking one

rows.Edge Case: Client stops taking prescribed medication without informing system, later mentions it in passing
rows.Rule: Re-run relevant protocol treating client as unmedicated/unmanaged for that condition until restated otherwise; do not assume prior "managed" status still holds

rows.Symptom: Chronic condition protocol conflicting silently with file 12 gate
rows.Cause: File 19 not checking file 12 precedence first
rows.Fix: Enforce Section 1's first IF branch (file 12 supersedes) as the mandatory entry check in every protocol call

rows.Symptom: Client's condition stability declining but program unchanged for weeks
rows.Cause: Section 5 monitoring not wired into check-in pipeline
rows.Fix: Ensure `monitorConditionStability()` runs on every processed check-in (file 11 Section 5), not just at intake

columns: System Confidence in "Safe to Proceed Normally"

rows.Signal Combination: Clear mild symptom, known benign pattern (e.g., typical DOMS)
Clear symptom matching known injury/condition table
rows.System Confidence in \"Safe to Proceed N: Depends entirely on that table's own branch (files 18/19)

rows.Signal Combination: Any ambiguity about whether a symptom is emergency-adjacent
rows.System Confidence in \"Safe to Proceed N: Always treated as needing escalation check
rows.Action: Route through file 12 Section 2 list check first, regardless of client's own casual framing of the symptom

rows.Edge Case: Client reports symptom casually mid-conversation, not as a formal "report" (e.g., "haha my chest felt weird earlier but I'm fine")
rows.Rule: Treat as a real report regardless of casual framing; run full classification, do not let tone downgrade triage seriousness

rows.Edge Case: Client reports symptom then immediately downplays or retracts it
rows.Rule: Log both the original report and the retraction; still apply Section 2/3 logic based on the original description, do not simply discard because client walked it back

rows.Edge Case: Multiple unrelated minor symptoms reported same day (e.g., mild knee ache + mild fatigue + slight dizziness)
rows.Rule: Classify and route each independently; do not average into a single "mostly fine" conclusion — the combination itself may warrant Section 2 dizziness branch escalation even if each alone seems minor

rows.Edge Case: Symptom reported by a third party (e.g., gym coach relaying "client mentioned chest tightness earlier")
rows.Rule: Treat with same seriousness as direct report; if any ambiguity about severity, default to requesting direct client confirmation before resuming normal session, per fail-conservative rule

rows.Symptom: Session proceeds normally despite an ambiguous health report
rows.Cause: `todaysSessionDecision()` default branch not wired to fail conservative
rows.Fix: Enforce Section 4's final ELSE to default to modified/light session, never full unmodified session, when action is unspecified

rows.Symptom: Repeated vague reports never escalate to a defined protocol
rows.Cause: Section 3 follow-up loop not re-invoking `classifyHealthReport()` after client answers
rows.Fix: Ensure structured answers feed back into Section 1 classification, not treated as a dead-end
